_metadata.description: Navico Hero Cases — Rich demo cases with full activity timelines for Copilot agent assist and supervisor view demos
scenarios: Scenario 1: Voice — Inbound B2B RMA (Atlantic Marine / Jake Harrington / Simrad NSX)
Scenario 2: Chat — Platinum Expert EMEA Escalation (Euro Marine / Christine Delacroix / B&G Triton2)
Scenario 3: Email — B2C Consumer Warranty (Mike Torres / Lowrance HDS Live)
Scenario 4: Supervisor View — Analytics & Queue Intelligence (Sarah Mitchell)

heroCases.title: Simrad NSX 3007 — Touchscreen Unresponsive After Firmware Update
heroCases.description:
Jake Harrington (Purchasing Manager, Atlantic Marine Supply Co.) reporting that Simrad NSX 3007 chartplotter (serial SIM-2024-NSX-10042) became completely unresponsive on the touchscreen following a firmware update to v3.8.1. Unit is installed on a 45ft commercial fishing vessel. Navigation is currently impaired.

Customer tier: Platinum Expert (B2B, Tier 1 Distributor). Auto-routed to Simrad specialist queue.

[DEMO FLOW: This case is pre-seeded as 'In Progress'. During demo, agent Emma receives incoming call from Jake. Screen pop surfaces this case + account 360. Copilot suggests KB-SIM-0042. Agent reviews, routes to Marco Rossi (Simrad Tier 4) for RMA validation.]

activities.subject: Inbound call — Jake Harrington re: NSX touchscreen failure
activities.description: Jake called in to report touchscreen issue. Left voicemail for Emma. Callback scheduled.

activities.subject: RE: Simrad NSX Touchscreen Issue — Case Reference NAV-2026-4412
activities.description: Auto-acknowledgment email sent to jake.harrington@atlanticmarinesupply.com confirming case creation and Platinum Expert SLA (2hr response).

activities.subject: Tech pre-screening — firmware v3.8.1 known issue
activities.description: Reviewed KB-SIM-0042. Unit serial SIM-2024-NSX-10042 is pre-Q3 2023 manufacture — higher failure rate flag. Warranty valid until 2027-04-15. Pre-approved for escalation to Marco Rossi (Simrad Tier 4).

copilotAssistHighlights: Suggested KB-SIM-0042 based on 'NSX touchscreen firmware' keywords
Drafted suggested reply with recovery steps
Flagged pre-Q3 2023 serial for elevated failure risk
Recommended RMA Exchange path based on case type + warranty status

heroCases.title: B&G Triton2 — Wind Instrument Erratic Before Race Season (URGENT)
heroCases.scenarioRole: HERO — Scenario 2 (Live Chat / Platinum Expert Escalation)
heroCases.description:
Christine Delacroix (Service Manager, Euro Marine Distributors, London EMEA) initiates chat from the Navico dealer portal. B&G Triton2 Instrument Display (serial BG-2024-TRI2-20011) showing erratic Apparent Wind Angle readings across 3 fleet units. Customer fleet preparing for spring racing season — resolution required within 48 hours.

Account tier: Platinum Expert (EMEA, Tier 1). Auto-escalated to priority queue with +1000 priority boost. Routed to Marco Rossi (B&G sailing specialist).

[DEMO FLOW: Christine chats in via portal widget. Copilot agent assist surfaces KB-BG-0117 instantly. Marco uses suggested reply. Issue not fully resolvable via chat — Copilot summarizes case for escalation. Supervisor Sarah can see conversation in Omnichannel Insights live.]

activities.subject: Chat context — customer portal initiated
activities.description: Christine initiated chat from dealer portal. Identified as Platinum Expert — auto-routed to Marco Rossi. Session started 09:14 GMT. Customer mentioned race season deadline (3 days).

copilotAssistHighlights: Surfaced KB-BG-0117 (Triton2 AWA calibration) within 8 seconds of case opening
Drafted opening suggested reply acknowledging Platinum Expert tier and urgency
Generated case summary for escalation handoff (when first-contact resolution not possible)
Notified supervisor Sarah Mitchell of Platinum Expert case in live queue

heroCases.title: Lowrance HDS-9 Live — Unit Not Powering On (Consumer Warranty Claim)
heroCases.scenarioRole: HERO — Scenario 3 (Email AI Triage / B2C Consumer)
heroCases.description:
Mike Torres (registered B2C consumer) sent inbound email to support@navico.com from mjtorres87@gmail.com. Subject: 'My Lowrance HDS wont turn on'. Body text: 'Hi, I bought my HDS 9 Live about a year ago and this morning it just won't turn on at all. I tried pressing the power button many times and checked the fuse but nothing works. Serial is on the back of the unit - LWR-2024-HDS-88821. Please help!'

AI triage: Intent = Warranty Claim. Product = Lowrance HDS-9 Live. Serial validated — warranty active (purchased Feb 2024, 3-year warranty expires Feb 2027). B2C consumer registered.

[DEMO FLOW: Inbound email arrives, AI creates case automatically, Copilot suggests KB-LWR-0088 and drafts reply with return instructions. Shows zero manual effort vs. today's manual email processing. NPS survey auto-queued on close.]
heroCases.inboundEmailText: Hi, I bought my HDS 9 Live about a year ago and this morning it just won't turn on at all. I tried pressing the power button many times and checked the fuse but nothing works. Serial is on the back of the unit - LWR-2024-HDS-88821. Please help!

activities.subject: My Lowrance HDS wont turn on
activities.description: Original inbound email from mjtorres87@gmail.com. AI extracted: product=Lowrance HDS-9 Live, issue=power failure, serial=LWR-2024-HDS-88821, intent=warranty claim.

activities.subject: RE: Your Navico Support Case — Warranty Claim #NAV-2026-4415
activities.description: [Copilot-drafted] Dear Mike, Thank you for contacting Navico Support. I have created a warranty claim case for your Lowrance HDS-9 Live (Serial: LWR-2024-HDS-88821). I've confirmed your unit is within the 3-year limited warranty. Please see the next steps below for your prepaid return...

copilotAssistHighlights: AI parsed inbound email — detected warranty claim intent, extracted serial number
Auto-created case, auto-populated serial, product, and customer fields
Surfaced KB-LWR-0088 — power troubleshooting + warranty claim steps
Drafted full consumer response with return instructions (one-click send)
NPS survey auto-queued for case close

heroCases.title: [SUPERVISOR VIEW] Queue Analytics & Intelligent Routing Dashboard
heroCases.scenarioRole: HERO — Scenario 4 (Supervisor Sarah Mitchell / Omnichannel Insights)
heroCases.description: Supervisor view demo showing Sarah Mitchell's experience as a Customer Service Manager at Navico. Demonstrates the connected intelligence that Sarah does NOT have today (single queue, manual categorization, no analytics).
supervisorDashboardHighlights: Live queue: 12 active conversations across email (6), chat (4), voice (2)
Agent utilization: Emma Thompson 85%, Marco Rossi 92% — Copilot suggest overflow routing
Case volume by brand: Lowrance 44%, Simrad 31%, B&G 18%, C-MAP 7%
Hot word alert: 'Navigation Failure' detected in new case — auto-bumped to priority queue
Cases awaiting categorization: 3 (down from 47 in current Navico state)
Copilot auto-categorization rate: 89% of cases categorized without manager review
SLA compliance: Platinum Expert 94%, Platinum 88%, Gold 76%
Backlog aging: 0 cases >24h for Platinum tier, 4 cases >8h for Gold
RMA queue: 6 pending tech validation, 2 awaiting ERP submission

before: 1 queue — all cases regardless of brand, tier, or skill
Managers manually reviewing and categorizing generic incoming cases
No visibility into agent utilization or queue health
RingCentral phone — no screen pop, manual account lookup every call
ServiceTarget KB (Rusty tech) — agents manually searching Q&A
NPS surveys ad hoc — no systematic follow-up
after: Intelligent omnichannel routing — email, chat, voice all unified
89% of cases auto-categorized by Copilot — managers focus on exceptions
Real-time supervisor insights — queue health, SLA, agent utilization
Screen pop on every inbound call — 0 seconds to customer context
Chat widget live on dealer portal — B2B customers self-serve or chat
Copilot agent assist — KB surfaces in <10 seconds, suggested replies
WFM preview available — shift scheduling, forecast, adherence
NPS auto-triggered on case close — full VOC loop
